entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Upport and Downport in SAP Using TCode UDO
entry.content:
Thanks for your response,
But it depends on client's system.
As i can see it is available in my system.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Access for Hybrid Landscape (No On-Premise Support)
entry.content:
The customer is currently out of SAP maintenance for all on-premise licensing and does not hold SAP Enterprise Support nor Product Support for Large Enterprises (PSLE).

However, the customer maintains active cloud contracts, including SAP Ariba, SuccessFactors, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), and Concur.

Given this scenario, how can the customer leverage SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: Re: SF to ECC Replication - Employee Org Assignment Data in Pending Status
entry.content: Hi Mark. I did'nt mean SLT. I have removed that tag. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Inbound Mail Processing in SAP
entry.content: @SRAVAN - Thanks for your reply.. you are talking about which mail server.

entry.title: Re: Event-driven architecture
entry.content:
The issue is resolved. The key point is that SAP Event Mesh only distributes events — it does not execute business logic or guarantee downstream processing. The correct approach is to let Event Mesh publish the event and then handle all processing in a dedicated consumer (SAP Integration Suite iFlow, Kyma service, CAP application, etc.).

Once I created a proper subscriber and moved the transformation/orchestration logic out of Event Mesh and into the consumer service, the event flow worked end‑to‑end. The event schema, queue subscription, and correlation handling were aligned, and the message was processed reliably.

In short: Event Mesh = transport layer Subscriber service = business logic + processing

entry.title: Output Parameters for Handling Unit
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I’m trying to configure HU printing for the Output Type HU_SHIPPING_LABEL.

My goal is to print the label only when using Pick-HU processing. In my scenario, the Pick-HU is:

  1. Created on the resource
  2. Gradually filled on the resource
  3. Finally placed into the Goods Issue bin   

What I want is to trigger the printing only when the HU is placed in the final destination bin.

The issue is the following:

  • If I use the HU creation step, the HU is still empty, so I do not have any content information available.
  • If I use the HU change/modification step, the output is triggered as soon as the HU is filled. If multiple products are placed into the HU, the label is printed:
    • once after confirming the product warehouse task
    • and a second time after the task that links the stock to the delivery

Additionally, if the HU contains several material/batch combinations, the system prints the full label every time the HU is updated. However, my requirement is to print the label only once, at the very end of the process.

I have already reviewed SAP Note 2941522 and the SAP documentation I could find, but I have not been able to solve it.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/36597ffd10024278bdb455c22bfd8c60.html

I also wanted to ask whether there is any way to get a determination log or trace for output determination. Without such a log, troubleshooting feels almost like trial and error, testing endless combinations until something finally works. On top of that, some fields seem to be incompatible with others during condition determination.

Has anyone faced the same issue or knows whether a determination log exists and how to access it?

Thanks in advance.

Vicente Jarque

entry.title: Re: Building Your First SAP Module Pool: A Step-by-Step Guide to Screen Painter & Menu Painter
entry.content:
It's a VERY OLD technology; the comments on this other blog post are still relevant: Module Pool Programming from scratch - SAP Community.

Note that your ABAP code is not formatted well. What I see:

What it should look like:

MODULE f4_cust_id INPUT.
  TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_INP,
           ID TYPE ZMP_LOGIN-pernr,
         END OF TY_INP.
  DATA: LT_VAL TYPE TABLE OF TY_INP.
  SELECT PERNR FROM ZMP_LOGIN INTO TABLE LT_VAL.
  CALL FUNCTION 'F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST'
    EXPORTING
      retfield  = 'CUST_ID'
      dynpprog  = sy-repid
      dynpnr    = sy-dynnr
      VALUE_ORG = 'S'
    TABLES
      value_tab = LT_VAL.
ENDMODULE.

entry.title: Re: Migration/Cut-over handling for open 107/109 Goods-in...
entry.content: Thank you for the feedback. I agree that the cleanest cut-over approach is probably to complete both 107 and 109 in the legacy system before migration, so that valuation and invoice processing are not duplicated in S/4HANA. The remaining issue is the physical availability after go-live. Some containers will still be on the vessel at cut-over, so the stock is financially owned but must not yet be used operationally in the receiving plant. Our idea is to migrate this stock into a dedicated storage location, e.g. “INTR”, which we already plan to use for transit-related processes. The stock should remain relevant for MRP, so that the system recognizes it as available supply and does not create new procurement proposals. However, it should not be available for picking, consumption, production, or sales until the goods physically arrive. As a cut-over activity directly after stock migration, we would create the stock transport order and immediately post the delivery/goods issue, so that the stock is already shown as stock in transit at go-live. When the container physically arrives, we would then post the goods receipt against the stock transport order into the final operational storage location. The purpose is to keep the stock financially migrated and MRP-relevant, but not physically available for operational use until arrival. Has anyone used this kind of approach in an S/4HANA migration cut-over?

entry.title: Error Notifications in "Manage Electronic Documents App"
entry.content:
Hi All

I am looking for detailed steps on how to automate the process of generating email for "Error Notifications" for Spain SII in "Manage Electronic Documents " App. Currently it seems to be a manual process and one has to log in to the app , check error and trigger the email. Is it possible to integrate this with a Batch Job or something? Has any one worked on such a requirement?

Thank

entry.title: Lessons Learned from Real SAP S/4HANA Migrations: A Technical Approach to Reducing Risk
entry.content:
Lessons Learned from Real SAP S/4HANA Migrations: A Technical Approach to Reducing Risk

Data migration is one of the most underestimated workstreams in SAP S/4HANA programs, even though it consistently carries some of the highest risk. After leading multiple multi‑region S/4HANA implementations, I’ve seen how quickly data issues can impact testing, cutover stability, and ultimately business continuity. What looks like a “technical activity” on paper often becomes the deciding factor in whether a program lands smoothly or struggles during critical phases.

Key Takeaways

  • Data migration risks are predictable — inconsistent legacy structures, missing attributes, and duplicates appear across nearly every S/4HANA program.
  • Early data profiling is essential — identifying structural gaps during design prevents major SIT/UAT disruptions.
  • Automation reduces manual effort and improves accuracy — record counts, balance checks, and anomaly detection should run every cycle.
  • Multiple mock cycles are critical — two cycles rarely provide enough stability; three or more significantly reduce cutover risk.
  • Centralized monitoring accelerates issue resolution — dashboards and exception logs improve coordination during high‑pressure cutovers.
  • Real project experience matters — successful programs treat data migration as a core transformation stream, not a technical afterthought.

Recurring Technical Challenges I See Across Programs

Across industries and regions, similar technical issues tend to surface:

  • Legacy systems with different data models and inconsistent field usage
  • Mandatory S/4HANA attributes missing in legacy master data
  • Duplicate business partners or materials that break BP conversion logic
  • Undefined reconciliation rules for record counts, balances, and deltas
  • Manual validation steps that don’t scale for high‑volume datasets

These issues aren’t theoretical — they show up in SIT, UAT, and especially during cutover when the pressure is highest.

Why Early Profiling Has Become Non‑Negotiable for Me

One of the biggest shifts in my approach has been pushing data profiling into the design phase. On a recent global rollout, we analyzed KNA1/KNVV data before mapping workshops even started. That early look exposed inconsistent account groups and missing regional assignments across business units. If we had discovered that during mock cycles, the remediation effort would have been far more disruptive.

Early profiling gives the team time to fix structural issues before they become blockers.

Early Data Profiling and Validation Workflow in SAP S/4HANA Migration Programs.

Illustrative example of early profiling, validation, and reconciliation activities performed during enterprise SAP S/4HANA migration cycles.

Anecdote 1: The Material Master Surprise

In another program, we ran an early profiling check on material master data and discovered that nearly 40% of materials across two regions were missing basic units of measure required by S/4HANA. The business assumed these fields were populated because they appeared in legacy reports — but the underlying tables told a different story.
Catching this during design prevented what would have been a major SIT blocker.

Automated Validation Frameworks Make a Measurable Difference

Manual validation will always have a place, but it cannot be the backbone of a large migration. In high‑volume programs, automation becomes essential.

Some of the checks that have consistently helped my teams include:

  • Source vs. target record count validation
  • Financial balance reconciliation (e.g., BSEG vs. ACDOCA)
  • Repeated‑failure detection across cycles
  • Pattern‑based anomaly detection for outliers, invalid dates, or blank clusters

These don’t require a full analytics platform — even lightweight scripts or ETL‑based checks can dramatically reduce manual effort and improve accuracy.

Anecdote 2: The Open Items That Didn’t Add Up

During one S/4HANA finance migration, automated balance checks flagged a recurring mismatch in open items for a specific company code. The business initially believed it was a rounding issue. But because the script highlighted the same discrepancy across three cycles, we dug deeper and found a legacy posting logic that had been quietly creating imbalance for years.
Without automation, this issue would have surfaced only during cutover — and it would have delayed go‑live.

Why I Push for More Migration Cycles Than Most Plans Include

Technically, two cycles look sufficient on a project plan. In reality, they rarely are.

Here’s how cycles typically play out:

  • Cycle 1: You discover the unknown issues
  • Cycle 2: You validate whether the fixes worked
  • Cycle 3+: You stabilize, optimize, and build confidence

The smoothest cutovers I’ve been part of had four or more cycles.

Migration Cycle Stabilization and Reconciliation Trend Example.

Illustrative example showing how repeated migration cycles improve reconciliation accuracy and reduce unresolved issues before cutover.

By the final cycle, the team could predict reconciliation results before the reports even ran — that’s when you know the migration is ready.

Cutover Monitoring: The Technical Backbone of a Calm Go‑Live

During cutover, visibility becomes the most important technical asset. In one multi‑region deployment, we implemented a simple dashboard that consolidated:

  • Failed load records
  • Reconciliation gaps
  • Mapping exceptions
  • Outstanding validation errors

It wasn’t a complex tool — but it transformed how quickly teams could respond. Instead of searching for information, they focused on resolving issues.

Sample Cutover Monitoring and Exception Tracking Dashboard.

Illustrative example of centralized monitoring used to track reconciliation gaps, failed loads, and validation exceptions during cutover activities.

That single change reduced cutover stress significantly.

Closing Thoughts

From a technical practitioner’s perspective, data migration is where the transformation meets real‑world complexity. Tools matter, but they don’t replace disciplined processes. What consistently reduces risk in S/4HANA programs is:

  • Early data profiling
  • Automated validation
  • Iterative migration cycles
  • Centralized monitoring and exception handling

These practices have made a measurable difference in every program I’ve led, and they continue to be the foundation of stable, predictable cutovers.

entry.title: Re: GL Role Restriction based on Company Code.
entry.content: Hi @ksiddiqui, as mentioned before, the screenshot I've previously sent was just for illustration. The configuration for hierarchies depends on your business rules, each company has its own, that’s why some have to use the KBA instructions to set restrictions properly. Please follow the resolution of this KBA(https://me.sap.com/notes/0003595981) --> Setting "Value Help" to "Restricted" [just like you did before with the other ones]. -> On the "General" tab set the company code restriction [as you've already performed on the write and read] -> Search for Company Code Hierarchies restriction [use the search tool below "Assigned Restriction Type"] -> To Maintain Restriction on the options "Company Code Hierarchy" and "Company Code Hierarchy Node" edit and select the company codes to restrict [click on the pencil on the right of each option.] [As on the right top corner you only have the options "Unrestricted" and "NOT Maintained" you must do it manually] -> Save.

entry.title: Re: Unable to get the Job packs created when "create work...
entry.content: Hi Koen, Yes, the issue is resolved for me. Please check the configuration in OPD for Maintenance order and Maintenance job packages. Make sure that you have similar settings maintained in both Maintenance order and Maintenance job packages. Its incomplete config for maintenance job package that causes this problem. I hope this helps. Thanks, Pavan Pilli

entry.title: Re: I want to know how to resolve real-time revenue errors.
entry.content:
Hello @db11 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your process, the root cause is most likely change you made to the account assignment during supplier invoice approval. The supplier invoice item is originally linked to the customer project but the approver changes the invoice detail and assign a fixed asset. This creates an account assignment situation that is not valid for real-time revenue recognition.

To put it in simple terms:

  • Event-based revenue recognition works based on posting to the relevant project account assignment object.
  • Postings to an account assignment object are considered by event-based revenue recognition.
  • A fixed asset acquisition is an Asset Accounting process, not a normal project cost posting for revenue recognition.
  • If the supplier invoice line is changed to an asset account assignment, real-time revenue recognition cannot handle it as a normal customer project cost event.
  • Reprocessing may allow the period-end process to continue but it does not remove the root cause. Note that Manage Real-Time Revenue Recognition Issues is used to view errors from automatic revenue recognition, for example when master data or process data causes an issue.

The clean way to handle it is to prevent supplier invoice items that are relevant for customer project revenue recognition from being changed to a fixed asset account assignment during approval. You should decide the business intent of the supplier invoice item if they are any of these:

  1. It is a project cost: If the supplier invoice is part of the customer project cost, keep the account assignment on the customer project/work package/WBS-related project object, depending on the app and process you use. Do not change the same invoice item to a fixed asset during approval.
  2. It is an asset acquisition: Do not treat it as a cost posting for customer project revenue recognition.
  3. It must be both project-related and capitalised: Then this should be designed as a controlled project capitalisation process, not by manually changing the supplier invoice approval line to a fixed asset account assignment. For this case, check whether your activate scope supports the required investment or asset capitalisation flow. If not, raise an SAP incident with the exact error details.

The monthly error will continue as long as the system still finds postings with an unsupported account assignment context for real-time revenue recognition. You can stop this by:

  • Identifying the supplier invoice items causing the error in Manage Real-Time Revenue Recognition issues.       
  • Checking the accounting document in Manage Journal Entries.                                                   
  • Confirm whether the line has fixed asset assignment instead of the intended project assignment.
  • Reverse and repost the supplier invoice correctly if the posting is wrong.
  • Adjust the supplier invoice approval process so approvers do not change project-relevant invoice lines to fixed asset assignment.
  • If the asset assignment is required by business design, exclude that posting from the customer project revenue recognition flow and process it through Asset Accounting instead.

Here are references for more guiding details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/afee0280b4c44b44b118634a87f9d3a7.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/6d56b5a5cf5d481d88097dc9e34f0a2d.html?locale=en-US

3041610 - Explanation for buttons in Revenue Recognition Fiori Apps - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Issues in Perform Maintenance Jobs
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

I have a few questions about this application. Questions 1 and 2 are particularly critical for me as they are affecting the process.  Thank you very much in advance.

1. When I try to record time confirmation using "SA" (Saat, which means Hour), the system gives the following error:

"Unit of measure H has not been created in language TR."

However, we do not use "H". We use the Turkish unit "SA" for hours. 

What is interesting is that the system allows me to save confirmations in Turkish when using dakika(DAK- which means minute). 

Also, if I  change the unit to another unit of measure and then switch back to "SA" (hours), the system allows the save successfully.

This behavior makes me think the issue may not actually be related to the missing of unit "H" in Turkish, because i can actually save with sa. Additionally, I do not receive this error when I select English as the system language or using IW41

2. When I try to enter a measurement and click save, the system does not save it, and I also cannot see the measurement entry in IK18.  When I save  measurement entry in another project, it is saved successfully. In this system, I was not able to activate the WDA measurement applications. Could this be related?

3. When I try to return part of a planned material, the system adds a new line below, and this line is also added to the order.

When I enter the quantity to be returned, the system does not immediately process or update it, and it still appears as 0. 

However, after entering the return quantity in the order, the “Post” button for the return line becomes active in the application, allowing me to complete the return.

Is this the expected process flow, or is there an issue here?

4.  I also receive this warning when using unplanned materials;

Is there really no way to return the material in this scenario? There should still be some way to process a return, shouldn’t there?

entry.title: Re: SQLMD: Cross Node Joins Analysis
entry.content:
@dirkhoffmann Host and Nodes are different columns in M_NUMA_NODES.

SQLMD asks for Nodes information. Whereas, your comment says the file should actually contain Host number information.

Could you please confirm once more if the file should contain Node or Host information?

My original question was, which System DB View from HANA, contains the information, that must be used to build the text file required to feed into SQLMD?

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Client 8.0 - SU01 tcode - roles tab
entry.content: This depends on your screen resolution. I see more than 3 roles on my test systems.

entry.content:

entry.title: Re: UNV to UNX Conversion guide: A step-by-step approach

Best, 

Eric

entry.title: Variable Pay (overall rating from indv. rating, company and department)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we are currently facing the following challenge in Variable Pay:

Our setup consists of Company Goals, Department/divison Goals, and an Individual Rating based on objectives + performance rating (pm form)

From these values, we calculate an overall achievement score, which is then used to derive payout guidelines.

Option 1:
We integrate both Company Goals and Department Goals in PMGM form and derive the overall achievement score there. This would allow us to easily connect the result to guidelines.

The challenge with this approach is handling mid-year organizational changes, where Department Goals cannot always be assigned correctly based on employee criteria. 

Option 2:
We map Company Goals and Department Goals directly in Variable Pay and calculate the overall achievement score using formulas. Based on this result, we would recreate the guideline logic with additional formulas and validation.

Has anyone implemented a similar setup or are there alternative approaches we should consider @xavierlegarrec ?

entry.title: Re: Building Your First SAP Module Pool: A Step-by-Step Guide to Screen Painter & Menu Painter
entry.content:
Hi @Sandra_Rossi 

Thank you for the feedback! You are completely right—Module Pool is definitely an older legacy technology, and modern SAP development has moved toward cleaner frameworks like RAP/CAP and Fiori for UI. I wrote this guide mainly to help developers who still have to maintain or touch older classic systems, as dealing with screen painter on day one can be a bit daunting!

Also, thank you for pointing out the formatting issue in the snippet. The platform's markdown editor scrambled the indentation a bit, so I will get that updated right away to make it easier to read.

Appreciate you taking the time to share your insights and the link to the other blog post!

entry.title: Fields in tableTCJ04
entry.content: table TCJ04 (containing the person responsible of WBE, under PS module) has field BLOCKED but it not possible to manage this field by customizing or by some user activity. My question is: "how this field works in the system"?

entry.title: Re: Required to Split Debit Entry in Sales Invoice Journa...
entry.content: Please go through my original suggestion where I have clearly asked to check ob40 also.  For your Account Key JOI, there should be a dedicated G/L Account assigned to show a separate line item in Financial postings.  This is also assuming that you set ERL for your pricing condition type

entry.title: Configuration of WDA PM applications
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am experiencing issues while activating PM WDA applications. The application screens are not loading properly, or the fields appear as disabled. This is the case for most WDA applications:

Create Maintenance Notification;

The “Change Maintenance Notification” screen appears to be the same as the create screen, as if the application is not separated into create, change, and display versions.

Create Measuring Point;

Create Maintenance Plan;

I have activated the relevant services via SICF, after checking them from the Fiori library. I have also activated some business functions, but this did not resolve the issue.

The applications seem as if they have not been fully installed or properly set up. Since same issue occurs across all applications,  I suspect that the root cause is the same. Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Re: Alert by email not sending SAP B1 Hana.
entry.content: It will not make any sense for topicstarter, but my case was in an absence of email address in Company details

entry.title: Re: Managing Pre-approvals using Attendance Quota in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi @neethadevaraj ,

Would you mind explaining in a bit more detail how you have configured the time type group "Payroll Relevant (PAYROLL_RLEVANT)" in your example ?  (in the 5th time valuation "CLT_THRESHOLD_TIMESHEET_WEEK")

I have been configuring the attendance quotas as you explain above, but when I configure this time type group as a "Time Pay Type", the system throws the error "The group PAYROLL_RLEVANT is influenced by a time container and can therefore only produce alerts or a flexible-period time container type as a result."

Yet, if I understand your blog correctly, it is required to configure some of the time type groups in the preceding chain as Time Container.

What am I missing ?

Thank you !

entry.title: Re: Open source PLC emulator for SAP EWM MFS in action
entry.content:
Great program! 

Does it happen to have a functionality to automatically reply to WT telegrams with a WTCO telegram?

entry.title: From XSUAA to AMS: Architecting S4HANA CAP Extensions with Policy-Based Authorization
entry.content:
Introduction

In modern SAP landscapes, extending S/4HANA is no longer just about building services or exposing APIs. The real complexity lies in how to secure enterprise extensions in a scalable and maintainable way.

Traditionally, SAP applications on SAP BTP have relied on XSUAA (XSUAA OAuth service) for authentication and authorization. While this model works well for scope-based access control, enterprise requirements often go beyond simple roles. They often involve business-context-based restrictions such as country, company code, or organizational unit.

As many of us, I have extensively used XSUAA to secure enterprise applications in SAP landscapes. In one of my recent projects, I had the opportunity to work with the IAS + AMS-based authentication and authorization model. That is when the WHY behind this architecture shift became clear.

As always, I wanted to share my learnings through a practical example - starting with the core concepts, followed by architectural differences and then a hands-on implementation.

The GOAL was not just to build another CAP application, but to understand a key architectural shift.

Let's get started by understanding the core design differences between the two authorization strategies

Core Architecture Differences

In SAP BTP - 
  • XSUAA has traditionally been the standard service for securing applications using scopes, role templates and role collections.
  • With AMS, the security model shifts toward a clearer separation between identity and authorization.

Let's start by understanding each model and key ideas behind it.

XSUAA Mental Model

Following diagram shows how the XSUAA model works in a simple way 

Figure-1

Key Ideas

  • IAS is the Identity Provider (IdP) responsible for authenticating users and establishing their identity.
  • XSUAA is the OAuth Authorization Server for the application, which issues JWT tokens containing scopes.
  • Authorization is scope-driven, where access control is enforced using scopes, role templates and role collections embedded inside the JWT token consumed by the application.

AMS Mental Model

Following diagram shows how the AMS model works in a simple way

Figure-2

 

Key Ideas

  • IAS is the Identity Provider (IdP) responsible for authenticating users and establishing identity.
  • AMS is the policy-based Authorization Engine that evaluates access rules based on roles, attributes and business conditions.
  • Authorization is policy-driven, where access is determined using configurable policies (e.g., country, company code) instead of static scopes.

Responsibility Shift

This comparison highlights a fundamental shift in how authorization is modeled: From static scopes defined at design time to dynamic policies evaluated at runtime.

AspectXSUAAAMS
Configuration filexs-security.json*.dcl
Authorization building blocksRoles + ScopesRoles + Policies + Attributes
Instance based data restrictionsLeads to role explosionStable roles + Dynamic policies
Change impactRedeploy neededPolicy update 
Runtime evaluationJWT scopesPolicy-driven (via ams plugin)

To make this architecture concrete, let’s walk through a simple vendor onboarding application that demonstrates how IAS, AMS and CAP work together in practice.

End-To-End Implementation: Hands-On

I have built a side-by-side extension - Vendor Onboarding application where:

  • Requesters can create and view vendor onboarding requests
  • Reviewers can approve or reject requests with comments
  • Role-based visibility is enforced using AMS policies to restrict data access

The following solution diagram illustrates the main components of the application

Figure-3

The above solution diagram illustrates the end-to-end architecture of the Vendor Onboarding application, including the CAP service, App Router, IAS authentication, AMS-based authorization and the SAP HANA persistence layer. It provides a high-level view of how the components interact in the overall system.

The complete GitHub repository for this implementation is publicly available and contains detailed documentation covering all aspects of the solution. In following sections, we will walk through some of the key AMS-related concepts and configurations used in this implementation.

AMS Authorization Policies

  • The cds add ams command generates an ams/dcl/ folder at the project root:
ams/dcl/
  schema.dcl                - AMS attribute schema (auto-generated; user-editable)
  cap/
    basePolicies.dcl        - auto-generated by cds build — DO NOT edit
  vendor/onboarding/
    reviewerPolicies.dcl    - hand-written app-based policies
  • Adding @restrict at the entity level is what triggers CAP's AMS plugin to generate basePolicies.dcl
@restrict: [
  { grant: ['READ', 'CREATE'], to: 'VendorRequester', where: 'requestedBy = $user' },
  { grant: ['READ', 'UPDATE', 'DELETE'], to: 'VendorReviewer' }
]
  • cds build translates this into
POLICY "VendorRequester" {
  ASSIGN ROLE "VendorRequester";
}

POLICY "VendorReviewer" {
  ASSIGN ROLE "VendorReviewer";
}

Instance Based Policies

To restrict VendorReviewers to specific countries, instance-based authorization is achieved by combining AMS schema attributes with policy definitions.
  • Defining AMS schema attributes: The first step is to expose relevant business fields as AMS attributes using CDS annotations. In this case, the country field is marked as an authorization attribute as shown below
@AMS.attributes: { country: (country) }
entity VendorRequests as projection on db.VendorRequests { ... }​
  • cds build then generates schema.dcl with: This registers country as a condition attribute usable in AMS policies.
SCHEMA {
  country: String
}
  • Write instance policies in ams/dcl/vendor/onboarding/reviewerPolicies.dcl:
POLICY "VendorReviewer_US" {
  ASSIGN ROLE "VendorReviewer" WHERE country = 'US';
}

POLICY "VendorReviewer_CA" {
  ASSIGN ROLE "VendorReviewer" WHERE country = 'CA';
}
 
This model enables fine-grained, instance-based authorization where the same CAP role (VendorReviewer) behaves differently depending on the policy context (e.g., country).

Instead of creating multiple roles per country, AMS allows authorization rules to be externalized into policies, improving scalability and maintainability.

The repository README contains the complete implementation details and configuration steps for this setup.

Wrap-Up

In this blog, we explored a practical implementation of a SAP CAP-based Vendor Onboarding extension secured using IAS and AMS and compared it with the traditional XSUAA-based security model.

The key objective was not just to build an application, but to understand the architectural shift in how enterprise authorization is modeled on SAP BTP.

With XSUAA, authorization is primarily scope-driven, where roles, role templates and role collections define access boundaries. While this model works well for standard role-based access control, it becomes rigid when enterprise requirements demand contextual restrictions such as country, organization, or cost center.

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my real world experience and learnings.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/java/ams
  2. https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-the-fundamentals-of-sap-system-security/introducing-sap-cloud-identity-services 
  3. https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/data-control-language-dcl

entry.title: Re: Eclipse/ ADT very slow
entry.content:
the idea was not to go faster, but to know what eclipse is trying to do when the scrollbar doesn't move. 

Like in Windows or Linux system, you could remove the beautiful logo at startup and have the detail of the actions executed. 

But the verbose mode of Eclipse seems not to be so verbous (maybe you could manage level of detail)

entry.title: Re: Displaying Status Icons in Each Row of SALV ABAP
entry.content: Nice practical guide. Showing status icons row by row in SALV is a simple but effective way to make report output much easier to scan, especially when users need to spot exceptions quickly. I like that the post keeps the solution focused on SALV’s built-in capabilities instead of adding unnecessary complexity, which makes it easier to reuse in real ABAP reports.

entry.title: Re: Reusable validation framework approach in UI5
entry.content:
Great write-up, Pooja!

This is a clean and practical approach to solving one of the most common UI5 pain points—duplicate validation logic scattered across multiple controllers. Moving the validation rules into a dedicated ValidationHelper.js not only improves reusability but also enforces better separation of concerns, which is something many UI5 projects struggle with.

A few things I especially liked:

  • Use of findAggregatedObjects to dynamically detect required fields. This keeps the solution generic and avoids hard‑coding control IDs.

  • Simple, readable API — validateRequiredFields(view) is intuitive and easy for any controller to adopt.

  • ValueState handling is consistent and user-friendly, which is key for form-heavy applications.

If you plan to extend this further, you might consider adding:

  • Pattern-based validations (email, phone formats, numeric ranges)

  • A central configuration file for validation rules

  • Support for custom messages per field

  • Integration with message popovers for multi-field error summaries

Overall, this is a solid, reusable pattern that many UI5 developers—especially those working on large enterprise apps—will find valuable. Thanks for sharing this!

entry.title: Re: Joule Everywhere: Breaking Down SAP’s Biggest AI Announcements from Sapphire 2026
entry.content: Great summary of SAP’s latest AI announcements—clear, concise, and captures exactly why Joule Everywhere is such a big shift for the ecosystem. Nicely done.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse/ ADT very slow
entry.content:
Thanks for the tip with the verbose mode, @FredericGirod !

Unfortunately I cannot see any relevant information:

 
09:29:04.255 [Start Level: Equinox Container: f640841e-8d0d-4116-8524-710166474806] DEBUG org.eclipse.jgit.internal.util.ShutdownHook -- register org.eclipse.jgit.util.FS$FileStoreAttributes$$Lambda/0x000002774f22a620@43c5d84 with shutdown hook
09:29:04.255 [Start Level: Equinox Container: f640841e-8d0d-4116-8524-710166474806] DEBUG org.eclipse.jgit.internal.util.ShutdownHook -- register org.eclipse.jgit.util.FS$FileStoreAttributes$$Lambda/0x000002774f22a620@43c5d84 with shutdown hook
09:29:06.530 [main] DEBUG org.eclipse.m2e.core.internal.project.registry.ProjectRegistryRefreshJob -- Queued refresh request: [/xxx_100_user1_de/pom.xml, /xxx_100_user1_de/pom.xml]
09:29:06.530 [main] DEBUG org.eclipse.m2e.core.internal.project.registry.ProjectRegistryRefreshJob -- Queued refresh request: [/xxx_100_user1_de/pom.xml, /xxx_100_user1_de/pom.xml]
Evaluating extension

entry.title: Re: Start Small. Think at Scale. Business Professionals Can Use SAP & Claude to Build a Business
entry.content: Nice angle. The post is valuable because it shows how SAP and Claude can help business professionals build a business case without needing a heavy technical setup. I like the “start small, think at scale” framing since it makes AI feel practical and approachable while still keeping an eye on broader enterprise impact.

entry.title: Re: From XSUAA to AMS: Architecting S4HANA CAP Extensions with Policy-Based Authorization
entry.content: Excellent breakdown of the shift from XSUAA to AMS. The policy‑based approach is clearly explained and really helps clarify how to architect secure, future‑proof S/4HANA CAP extensions. Great contribution.

entry.title: Re: Building an LLM Agent using CAP
entry.content: Great tutorial. It’s helpful to see an LLM agent built directly with CAP and connected to AI Core through the SAP AI SDK, because that makes the architecture feel much more enterprise-ready. I also like that the post focuses on a simple end-to-end example, which makes the agent pattern easier to understand before adding more complex tools or workflows.

entry.title: Re: Stay certified assessement for C_TS4FI_2023
entry.content:
Hi Thomas,

Please refer to this learning journey to prepare for the assessment questions: 
Applying Business AI Solutions and Expertise in SAP Finance 

Good luck!

entry.title: Re: SAP Document AI in SAP MDG using Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM)
entry.content:
@ adityaIngale66

No waiting for a response from the blog author mohamedalijinna_nainamoha.

But the SAP standard DIE scenario is working in our onpremise system very well.

entry.title: Re: User Research Method Cards: Available for Download
entry.content: Hi, Link https://www.sap.com/aemedge/assets/fiori-design-web/v1-130/user-research-resources/2024_SAP_UserResearch_MethodCards.pdf isn´t working anymore! Do you have another one on hand?

entry.title: Re: What is the Use of Private Environment in SAP Build P...
entry.content:
The private environment is basically your own isolated workspace for SAP Build Process Automation. It lets you deploy and run projects with tighter security, controlled access, and separate resources instead of sharing everything in the public/default environment.

Why it matters

A private or shared environment is useful when different teams, business areas, or projects should not see or use the same artifacts, triggers, agents, or API keys. SAP’s documentation says environments are the functional area where you deploy and run projects, and shared environments increase security by limiting access and managing project members.

In practical terms

  • You can keep automation projects separated by business domain, such as finance, procurement, or HR.

  • You can assign only the right users, agents, and artifacts to that environment.

  • You can deploy different project versions in different environments without mixing them up.

When to use it

Use a private/shared environment when you want better governance, safer testing, or clean separation between projects and teams. It is especially helpful when you do not want every user in the tenant to access the same processes or automations.

entry.title: Need Guidance to configure Push notification scenarios.
entry.content:
Hello,

Need Guidance to configure Push notification scenarios.

Push for supervisor:-

When Technician Create a Notification using SSAM or GUI.

When Technician Submit the work for review using SSAM.

Push For Technicians

When any operation assigns to technician.

entry.title: Re: Setting up Python in SAP Business Application Studio to trigger HANA Cloud Machine Learning
entry.content: Great walkthrough—simple, clear, and super helpful for anyone looking to trigger HANA Cloud from Python in BAS. Thanks for breaking it down so cleanly.

If not, does SAP otherwise support that legal requirement?

Cheers J

entry.title: Re: Integrating Google Gemini AI with SAPUI5 Frontend using Node.js Backend
entry.content: Nice end-to-end example. Integrating Gemini with a SAPUI5 frontend through a Node.js backend is a practical way to keep the UI simple while handling AI calls securely on the server side. I like that the post shows both text generation and image analysis, because it makes the setup feel useful beyond a basic chatbot demo.

entry.title: Re: How to Generate and Download a Dynamic PDF from SAPUI5 Form Data Using Node.js
entry.content: Nice example-driven walkthrough. The dynamic PDF generation flow is clean, practical, and easy to reuse in real UI5–Node.js scenarios. Thanks for sharing this approach.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - CI - Immutable Event Routing per Domain with Metadata-Driven Consumer Orchestration
entry.content: Very strong architectural pattern. The post shows how to build a metadata-driven Smart Router in SAP BTP CI so that routing decisions for events are resolved at runtime using Value Mapping and Groovy, instead of redeploying artifacts whenever a new consumer or domain is added. That’s exactly what makes the event routing “immutable” — the deployed iFlow stays the same, and only the metadata changes.

entry.title: Re: Evolving Our SAP Signavio Value Accelerators: Announcing Upcoming Content Updates Q2 2026
entry.content: Great update. The upcoming enhancements to the Signavio Value Accelerators look genuinely impactful—especially the expanded industry content and deeper integration across the transformation suite. Excited to see this evolve.

Please see the screenshot below

entry.title: Re: Fields in tableTCJ04
entry.content:
First, check in sm30, input V_TCJ04 and see whether you are able to edit this field.

If not, go to se11, input TCJ04 and display. Ensure the Delivery Class is C and Data Browser/Table View Editing is set to "X"

Next execute SOBJ transaction and check the following for Object V_TCJ04

  • "C" for Type
  • Category - CUST
  • Current Settings is ticked

I am not sure the above will work for you as I never did this. Try and update here

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Deprecation of Data Attribute Recommendation...
entry.content:
Hi,

As suggested in the note, you can use SAP-RPT-1 instead of Data Attribute Recommendation. SAP-RPT-1 can be tested for free at the SAP RPT playground available at https://rpt.cloud.sap .

Best regards,
Francesco

entry.title: Re: Materials are getting skipped during MRP live(MD01N) run
entry.content: Thanks Lakshmipathi for your reply. We checked all these points and not applicable for us. e.g. packet size we are using is 1000 but at hana live level it is 10000. Locking and etc. are checked, no other activity is being performed in the system. This skipping of articles is happing every time and not just one time.

entry.title: Re: Convergent Invoicing SSCUIs not visible in Configurat...
entry.content:
Hi byd,
thank you for your detailed questions and for describing your scenario clearly. Let me address your points below:

a) SSCUIs availability in CBC
The SSCUIs you listed are part of the Convergent Invoicing (CI) configuration scope.

These SSCUIs generally become available once the relevant Convergent Invoicing scope is activated. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, this is typically linked to scope item 2BG. After activation of 2BG, the majority of the SSCUIs you listed should become visible in CBC.
An exception to this is 103286 – Define Residence Time for Billable Items. This is not exposed in CBC in the same way as the others and therefore will remain unavailable.


b) Role of Convergent Invoicing & your manufacturing use case
Your understanding of Convergent Invoicing is technically correct in principle: It consolidates multiple billing sources into a single invoice.

However, in the context of S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is an important product direction to be aware of:
Currently, there are no plans to support integration from Sales Billing to Convergent Invoicing in S/4HANA Public Cloud. The business need to combine physical goods and subscription or service charges is already covered by standard Sales Billing capabilities.

Relevant scope items for this scenario are Omnichannel Convergent Billing (1MC) & Subscription Management with Sales Billing (57Z). These scope items allow:
- Integration of subscription and service billing directly into SD billing
- Creation of combined invoices within SD

For more information please review influence request 294573 - SD to SAP CI integration to create convergent invoice in S/4HANA Public Cloud.


Best regards,
Daniel

entry.title: Re: Need to upgrade the tomcat version embed in CR2020.
entry.content:
Sorry but you are wrong sravan, CR Designer does not install Tomcat. Crystal Reports Server however does. You are correct about upgrading, if using CR Server it is the only way to upgrade Tomcat.

FYI - Crystal Reports uses Java Scripts, doesn't require Tomcat or any other Java WEB Server.

entry.title: VIES Validation with Multiple VAT IDs
entry.content:
SAP Support Request: VIES Validation with Multiple VAT IDs

Affected Component Component: FI-LOC-OVF (Online Validation Framework) Subcomponent: VIES Validation

Problem Description Brief Summary: SSCUI 105630 allows only one VAT ID for VIES validation with multiple subsidiaries

Detailed Description: Our company has multiple subsidiaries in various EU countries, which are represented by separate company codes in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud. Each subsidiary has its own VAT identification number. However, the VIES validation system in SSCUI 105630 "Maintain VIES specific parameters" only allows the storage of a single "Requestor's VAT Number". This leads to limitations in proper VAT validation of EU business partners, as not all subsidiaries can be correctly represented as requestors. A valid requestor VAT ID is required for VIES requests to obtain consultation numbers.   

How could we deal with this? Are there already solutions for it?

entry.title: Re: Certification Exam System Unavailable
entry.content: I'm facing the same problem, were you able to solve it?

entry.title: Re: Fields in tableTCJ04
entry.content:
Additionally, there is a maintenance view to block the person responsible: V_TCJ06 (Person Responsible Blocking).

  • Note: There does not appear to be an associated Customizing option for this functionality (AFAIK)

entry.title: Re: LMS notifications
entry.content: Just adding on top of what Duda has suggested, in some cases we have also observe the behaviour of footer image needing to be downloaded or permitted due to the "From" email notification server has not been whitelisted by the organisation's IT/Security team. Some information can be found by here (https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/managing-sap-successfactors-learning-for-administrators/email-domain-validation-using-domain-keys-identified-mail-dkim?locale=en-US&version=LATEST), or search SuccessFactors Learning DKIM, which should help on the set up. Thanks, Sunny

entry.title: Contextualize and reason Post-SAP Sapphire SAP Business Data Cloud briefing
entry.content:
AI pilots are easy.  Scaling trusted AI across the enterprise is where the real work starts.

Join our post-SAP Sapphire briefing for SAP Partners and learn how SAP Business Data Cloud powers the Autonomous Enterprise.

We’re covering:
• SAP BDC and Knowledge Graph
• Joule and Joule Studio
• AI Agent Hub and partner motions
• Faster migrations and governed AI
• New SELL, SERVICE, and BUILD opportunities

Plus, we’ll connect the technology to customer outcomes that matter: lower complexity, smarter automation, and faster decision-making across SAP and non-SAP systems.

One detail that stands out?  Customers are asking for AI they can trust, not disconnected copilots that create more noise.

This session helps you position the bigger story and turn it into action with your customers.

Register now and bring the Autonomous Enterprise conversation into every account.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/may/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014197.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPTechEd #AIAgents #SAPData #FutureOfEnterprise

entry.title: Re: Understanding Single Sign On (SSO) in SAP BTP with IAS and Microsoft Entra ID
entry.content: @shoeb_k79 , Very informative post. It would be great if you share the Part 2 soon.

entry.title: Re: Powerdesigner: Comment obtenir les template excel de...
entry.content: I have a complete example for a CDM/LDM, which you can find on GitHub (SAP will not allow me to attach it) - https://github.com/MetadataJunkie/PowerDesigner_Shared/blob/da7e2dd71c2d7f99547582efe0a957cdda346f7e/Excel%20Imports/Import%2006%20-%20Whole%20model.xlsx

entry.title: Build Process Automation navigating SAP GUI to Excel
entry.content: I'm currently working on a project in BPA where we are automating the process of downloading income statements from multiple profit centers in SAP, compiling them in a single excel file as sheets, and then uploading them to a folder in a SharePoint. Currently I've set it up so that the bot navigates the SAP GUI u, inputs the report group, and will fill out the information for one profit center. I changed environment settings so the output is a Microsoft Excel 2007 since I was getting internal macro errors with just Microsoft Excel. The report runs and opens in excel, but from there is where we are having trouble. I've tried capturing excel using the BPA capture tool, and also adding it as an application, but when I ran the "Get Open Notebooks" activity it still wouldn't recognize the open excel workbook.  Any pointers on how I should go about this? We've also tried downloading the files directly, but that messes with the formatting and they become messy when opening as an excel that way. I've tried finding videos on connecting excel with BPA, but most of the content out there focuses on opening new excel workbooks and doesn't have much on how to connect to existing ones that SAP GUI opens. Any help on the best way to build this pipeline would be much appreciated. Thank you.

entry.title: Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise: A 6-City North America Tour for Architects
entry.content:
The announcements coming out of Sapphire this year were significant. Joule Work. The SAP Autonomous Suite. The SAP Business AI Platform. The SAP Family of Models. AI agents becoming first-class citizens inside enterprise landscapes.
 
For Enterprise Architects, this is not just another product update cycle. This is a fundamental shift in how businesses are designed, governed, and operated. And it is happening fast, faster than any of us can fully process alone.
 
That is why we are thrilled to announce that we are taking the Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise experience on the road this summer for a complimentary 6-city in-person learning tour across North America.
 
CityDate
Palo Alto18 Jun 2026
Atlanta23 Jun 2026
Dallas25 Jun 2026
Toronto14 Jul 2026
Newtown Square16 Jul 2026
Calgary21 Jul 2026
 
👉 In-person tour registration: events.sap.com/eaa-aet/en_us/home.html

This is an architect-to-architect event. No sales pitches. Just practitioners, thought leaders, customers, and community working through what this era of transformation means in practice, together.

What We Will Explore Together

Each city event is structured around eight core sessions designed to take you from strategic clarity through to practical architectural guidance:
 
  1. SAP Sapphire Key Takeaways for Enterprise Architects : What Matters and Why We cut through the noise. What were the announcements that genuinely change the architectural picture, and what are the implications for how you plan, design, and govern your enterprise landscape going forward?
  2. Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise : What It Is, Why It Matters, and How to Prepare Before you can architect toward a destination, you need to understand what it actually is. We ground the vision of the Autonomous Enterprise in concrete architectural terms : what changes, what stays the same, and where the real decisions lie.
  3. Decoding SAP's Evolving Portfolio : Implications for Enterprise Architecture SAP's portfolio is evolving rapidly. This session maps the strategic shifts — from SAP Autonomous Suite to the Business AI Platform — and draws out the direct implications for enterprise architects responsible for modernising, integrating, and governing complex SAP landscapes.
  4. Designing the AI-Native Enterprise : Defining Your North Star Architecture Every transformation journey needs a destination. This session helps you define your North Star Architecture for the AI-native enterprise — a forward-looking reference model that guides investment decisions, platform choices, and integration patterns in an agentic world.
  5. The AI Golden Path : Developing AI Applications Across the SAP Ecosystem The Golden Path is how you move from strategy to execution. We explore the recommended patterns, platforms, and practices for developing, deploying, and scaling AI applications across the SAP ecosystem, giving you a repeatable approach that works at enterprise scale.
  6. Who Governs the Agents? AI Governance at Scale As AI agents proliferate across enterprise landscapes, governance becomes the defining architectural challenge. This session tackles agent lifecycle management, security, compliance, trust, and the guardrails that allow organisations to move fast without losing control.
  7. The Enterprise Architect's Role in the Autonomous Enterprise — Leadership, Influence, and Community The role of the Enterprise Architect is being redefined. This session explores how EAs position themselves not just as technical designers but as strategic leaders driving AI adoption, shaping governance frameworks, and influencing transformation at the board level.
  8. Architect-to-Architect Open Forum : What We're Excited About, What We're Missing, and What Comes Next This is the session I personally look forward to most at every stop. No slides. No script. Just an open, honest conversation between architects , about what is working, what is not, what the community is excited about, and what we still need to figure out together.

 Where We Are Heading

We will be visiting six cities across North America this summer. Each event is complimentary and designed to give you a full day of learning, connection, and conversation:
📍 Palo Alto — June 18, 2026
📍 Atlanta — June 23, 2026
📍 Dallas — June 25, 2026
📍 Toronto — July 14, 2026
📍 Newtown Square — July 16, 2026
📍 Calgary — July 21, 2026

Bonus Morning Sessions — Going Even Deeper

For those who want to go further, each city also features an optional complimentary morning learning experience before the main event:
 
🔐 Security, AI and Governance Across SAP Cloud Environments Available in: Palo Alto · Atlanta · Dallas · Calgary A focused deep dive into how organisations design and enforce security, privacy, compliance, and clean-core principles across SAP and non-SAP environments in an AI-native world.
 
📊 Data Inspired: Street-Smart Data Literacy Available in: Toronto · Newtown Square Inspired by Dr. Sebastian Wernicke's acclaimed book and TED Talk, this morning session brings practical data literacy to life for architects and transformation leaders — helping you ask better questions, make sharper decisions, and build organisations that genuinely run on data.
 
The pace of change around AI, Agentic AI, and the Autonomous Enterprise is accelerating faster than any of us can keep up with individually.
 
The architects we speak with every week are brilliant, curious, and deeply committed to leading their organisations through this transformation but they are often doing it in isolation, working through the same questions, hitting the same walls, solving the same problems independently. That has to change.
 
The best thinking will come from a conversation in a corridor, a whiteboard session that ran forty-five minutes over, a question from the back of the room that reframed everything.
 
That is what this tour is designed to create. Not just learning, but community feeling.
 
There has never been a more exciting time to be an Enterprise Architect. And there has never been a more important time to be connected to others who are navigating this journey alongside you.
 
For those who cannot join us in person, please feel free to join us online on our webinar series (see below).

Register Now for the In-person Experience — It Is Complimentary

Both the in-person tour and the online series are completely free to attend.  All you need to do is register.
 
👉 In-person tour registration: events.sap.com/eaa-aet/en_us/home.html

The Online Webinar Series - Register Below

If you cannot join us in person or if you want to go even deeper on the technical and architectural details we are also running a companion 11-part online webinar series, Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise, every week from 1st June through to 12th August 2026.
 
Covering Joule Work, the SAP Business AI Platform, AI Agent Lifecycle Management, the SAP Family of Models, Data Foundations, Governance, and much more — it is the same architect-to-architect spirit, delivered globally and available live and on-demand.
👉 Full series details and registration: events.sap.com/eaa-autent-web/en_us/home.html
 
I am looking forward to reconnecting with familiar faces and meeting new architects across our ecosystem. We would love to hear what you are working on, what is keeping you up at night, and where you think this all goes next.
 
Thank you & Regards,
 
Paul Kurchina, Ralph Richter & Gaurish Dessai

entry.title: Manager rating also updated when selecting employee self rating in self evaluation - Latest PM forms
entry.content:
Hi All,

While testing the latest experience of PM forms, during self-evaluation step, when employee selects a self-rating, in the goal detail window, the manager rating field is also updated with the same rating in the summary and detail screen. But this goes off once you save the form or the form gets autosaved. Please see screenshot below. This does not happen when an employee self rating is selected from the summary screen. Has anyone else faced this issue? 

Thank you,

Meera

entry.title: Re: VIES Validation with Multiple VAT IDs
entry.content: Have you verified whether the VIES (VAT Information Exchange System) offers an API capable of processing multiple values at onc

entry.title: Re: ByDesign Supplier Invoice API - ManageSupplierInvoiceIn
entry.content: Try removing this tag - <GeneralLedgerAccountCode>400000</GeneralLedgerAccountCode>

entry.title: Re: Hi All, I am facing an issue with FAGLF101.
entry.content:
@gunesh_dahake32 For verification purposes, could you provide screenshots of the checks you executed (OBBU, OB59, etc.)
Valuation Method Verification StepsStep Action Purpose

1. Verify Valuation Method AssignmentGo to OBBU and confirm that your valuation method (e.g., YVR) is correctly assigned to the company code and valuation area.Ensures the system knows which valuation method applies.
2. Check Valuation ProcedureIn OB59, verify that the valuation procedure is linked to the correct valuation method and valuation area.Confirms that the valuation procedure is active for your setup.
3. Review Account DeterminationGo to OBYC and check the relevant transaction key (e.g., KDM, KDX, KDB, depending on your valuation type).Ensures G/L accounts are maintained for valuation postings.
4. Maintain Missing G/L AccountIn OBYC, for the transaction key causing the error, maintain the missing account 1201110 for your company code and valuation area.Fixes the missing account mapping.
5. Validate Chart of AccountsConfirm that account 1201110 exists in your chart of accounts and is assigned to the company code.Prevents "account missing" errors.
6. Test PostingRe-run the valuation or posting transaction to confirm the error is resolved.Verifies configuration correctness.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere: To Slash or to Backslash? Resolving the Compound Characteristic Crisis
entry.content: Hi @BenedictV , thanks for your vote and comment! I also ask myself how this happened... The business content in Datasphere had the forward slash. I didn't notice it until someone tried to upload data with backslash to SAC (planning model). This person showed me, that data "in SAC" (via DSP Analytic Model) is represented with backslash - at least sometimes 😅

entry.title: Re: Access Performance Forms Directly from SAP SuccessFactors Stories: New in 1H 2025!
entry.content:
Hi,

does this feature respect RBPs? Or does the employee having access to the report see everybodies performance form?

Kind regards,

Adrian

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 8 license doesn't allow subreport man...
entry.content: Thank you so much for the feedback. Getting an RCAPI license would be the only solution that would work IMO. Any information on how to get my hands on one would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: I tricked Datasphere into running my custom scaler functions
entry.content:
Thanks for this article
For sure it can open several ways to enhance dsp capabilities

I hoped this could help me to force a pulldown from source database.
With calculated variables or join, Datasphere selects the source data, and then filter, which is too long in my case (I need to dynamicaly filter on 3 date fields)
With these scalar functions, result is not quickest unfortunately. Datasphere still selects the source data, and then filter,

Another issue : I couldn't persist a view with functions:
" no definition found for DAC 'SANDBOX.GET_AS400_YEAR'" (maybe because I had to remove DETERMINISTIC)

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Adding Attachments to a Standard XML
entry.content:
Hello, we are implementing S4 Hana Public Cloud too, in our case for Spain. Can you please confirm we could implement it as well?

Many thanks

Santiago

entry.title: Re: New here!
entry.content: Welcome! And nice to meet you! I'm working on my SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Certification. Pretty excited about this journey. Looking forward to some great discussions.

entry.title: Re: Managing Invoices with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management (now SAP Ariba Invoicing)
entry.content:
SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (SAP DRC)

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is SAP’s centralized, end-to-end solution for managing global tax, e-invoicing, and statutory reporting. It automates the extraction, structuring, and submission of financial data directly to tax authorities, helping businesses stay legally compliant across multiple countries.

The community topic is the one stop shop for all information related to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.
This page covers video tutorials, YouTube video library on the topic, session recordings, blogs, Partner collaboration and Solutions, reports, SAP Help documentations, learning sessions, event details where the topic was discussed, etc.

  • SAP Community Topic - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

Upcoming Live Sessions on DRC

entry.title: Re: Invoicing in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (SAP DRC)

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is SAP’s centralized, end-to-end solution for managing global tax, e-invoicing, and statutory reporting. It automates the extraction, structuring, and submission of financial data directly to tax authorities, helping businesses stay legally compliant across multiple countries.

The community topic is the one stop shop for all information related to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.
This page covers video tutorials, YouTube video library on the topic, session recordings, blogs, Partner collaboration and Solutions, reports, SAP Help documentations, learning sessions, event details where the topic was discussed, etc.

  • SAP Community Topic - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

Upcoming Live Sessions on DRC

entry.title: Re: I tricked Datasphere into running my custom scaler functions
entry.content:
@aclauin 

the problem with pushdown to the remote source is with DPagent and abap adapter ( remote table with DPAgent connected to an ABAP source system)? 

in this case there is nothing you can do, this is a known limitation of the adapter...

the workaround is : translate the date into integer in the source system CDS / table / view and then the filter can be pushed down (only if it is with '=' clause).

entry.title: Re: Introduction - Liilet Sanders SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Certification
entry.content: Hi Liilet! Nice to meet you! I'm working on my SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Certification. Pretty excited about this journey. Looking forward to some great discussions.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 8 license doesn't allow subreport man...
entry.content: Don, any help with obtaining an RCAPI, even if it costs money, would be the best solution. Thanks so much for the help.

entry.title: Error creating Purchase Requisitions
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We are experiencing a error when creating Purchase Requisitions.

The problem occurs with certain materials that have a price of 0 in the material master data (transaction MM03). These materials are intentionally maintained with a zero price because they are non-stock managed materials.

According to the Performance Assistant / field help, the price field should not be mandatory. However, in practice, the system requires the field to be filled in order to continue.

When we leave the field empty, the system does not allow us to proceed. If we enter 0 as the price, the system throws an error and the Purchase Requisition cannot be submitted.

We would like to know:

  • Is there any configuration or parameterization in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that allows Purchase Requisitions to be created with a price of 0?
  • How can this behavior be adjusted so that materials with a permanent zero price can be processed correctly?
  • Is there any standard configuration or best practice recommended by SAP for this scenario?

Thank you in advance.

Greetings

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts
entry.content: How can I get free access to SAP ERP as a student?

entry.title: Re: Name 3 field in the Bank data of BP
entry.content:
Hi Rahgu,

Good Day.

The field NAME3 is not an attribute provided in the BUT0BK entity thus it is not available in the Bank UIBB.  Please see SAP Note 2221398 - MDG-BP/C/S/CA: (Un-)Supported Fields in Data Model BP. 

Thank you.

entry.title: Qualification rule is not working correctly
entry.content:
Hello everyone. I hope you're all well.

I'm setting up an eligibility rule for the bonus plan.

The idea behind the rule is that employees with less than 3 months at the company will not be eligible.

 

However, as you can see in the screenshot below, I have a record that does not meet this requirement, but ends up passing the rule as eligible.

 

The rule has other requirements, but these are okay. The eligible employees comply with the guidelines of the rule. The problem arose with this last point, regarding the minimum time.
I also checked her profile to see how her registration is set up, and everything is correctly applied. No empty fields or any inconsistencies.
I also did some other tests that made this registration ineligible. However, other people were also made ineligible, which is not correct.

Thank you in advance for your time and help.

@xavierlegarrec and community

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay (overall rating from indv. rating, compa...
entry.content:
Hi @Gunnar92 

I would take Option 2, it's more difficult technically to build in varpay but it helps keeping Company and Department goals in the Varpay template where they should be. No need to put things in PM when we can have them in VarPay with a twist in the config.

Thanks

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Change "Open on Key date" in the "Overdue receivables...
entry.content:
Dear @FabianSCC,

The Open on Key Date field in the Overdue Receivables app is based on date functions, not on unrestricted manual date entry. The standard app allows the user to choose from existing key dates/date functions for example TODAY, YESTERDAY, FIRSTDAYOFTHEYEAR, etc. SAP Public Cloud CDS documentation for Overdue Receivables confirms that the input parameter is P_DateFunction and that date functions can be maintained with Manage Date Functions

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/809e5e5596c24e4b8505c2ff99379c55.html

If a specific reusable key date is needed the supported approach is to create or maintain an appropriate date function in Manage Date Functions

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2595A')/S36

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay (overall rating from indv. rating, compa...
entry.content: Thanks you so much for the swift reply - as always!!

entry.title: Recognizing Educational Excellence: UCS Honored at SAP Academic Community Conference LAC 2026
entry.content:
[This blog post is the translation in LinkedIn from the original post in English by Mateus Panizzon, from University UCS, Brazil: Post | Feed | LinkedIn ]  

I’m grateful to share that UCS - University of Caxias do Sul > UCS Business School received the SAP University Alliances Excellence Award 2026/2027 (Education) at the #SAP Academic Community Conference LAC 2026.

The SAP Excellence honors outstanding lighthouse institutions in the SAP University Alliances network, in three categories: #education, #research, and #innovation.

The award process involved submitting a report on the last years of SAP University Alliances initiatives at UCS, including the adoption of SAP technologies in undergraduate, specialization, and graduate programs; participation in events; missions to SAP Experience Centers; joint publications; and industry collaboration.

The report was blind-reviewed by the Academic Board in Germany and Asia-Pacific (APJC).

This recognition is the result of the efforts of many people who bring the SAP Academic Alliance Program to life: SAP and UCS industry partners, especially, Randoncorp, who motivated us since the very beginning, several faculty managers and coordinators,  professors, and especially the students — the future talents at the center of this process. This award is for all of you.

I would like to thank María Cecilia Calzada de Neumann and extend my gratitude to the entire SAP University Alliances team for all the learning throughout this journey. From Victor Fagundes, to Gloria Sandi and Isadora Royer + Ariadne Bessa Rodrigues, as well Prof. Alberto Medeiros for the mentoring, thank you for always keeping us connected and engaged with this inspiring community.

As always, the LAC event was a high-level initiative, bringing together and strengthening a community that is helping shape the future of #education with #technology. The real difference of University Alliances is that it is not only about #software — although we have access to technologies ranging from SAP Business Builders to real SAP applications — but about #people and what we can cocreate together in ways that make pedagogical sense for our reality.

The event also brought important updates on plug-and-play resources designed to enhance student learning and make the adoption of SAP #AI technologies more accessible, practical, and meaningful in the classroom.

The conference was also a valuable opportunity to meet Katharina Schäfer, Global Head of Academic Partnerships at SAP, in person and learn from her perspectives on the future of the UA program, especially regarding strategies for student engagement, portfolio ambidexterity and balancing the #digital transition, faculty engagement in the new platform, and #skills of the future. ☕

On a personal level, mobilizing a University Alliances Program is no easy task. It is time-consuming, but also keeps us connected to cutting-edge concepts and technologies from a #global industry #leader. I am grateful for the opportunity to carry out this mission during this cycle, and hope we can continue building great things together with the UA Community🙏

More to come,
